trade.summary: China is South Korea's largest trading partner. South Korea exports semiconductors, petrochemicals, vehicles, and displays. China exports electronics, machinery, and chemicals. The trade relationship is vital but increasingly tense due to competition in key industries (displays, batteries, EVs) and security tensions.
disputes: THAAD deployment fallout (2016-2017) ongoing economic effects
South Korean concerns about Chinese competition in chips, displays, and EVs
Import restrictions on K-culture (Hallyu) during THAAD dispute
Technology competition in batteries and semiconductors

diplomatic.summary: Relations remain significantly strained following the THAAD controversy of 2016-2017. China imposed informal economic sanctions (tourism boycott, retail restrictions on Korean brands). Relations formally normalized but distrust persists. South Korea under President Yoon (2022-2025) tilted strongly toward the US-Japan alliance, deepening China's concerns. Korea's involvement in semiconductor export controls has further strained ties.
recent_incidents: THAAD economic retaliation legacy still affecting Korean tourism to China
South Korea joined US-led semiconductor export controls
North Korea-China alignment vs South Korea-US-Japan alignment deepening
South Korean domestic impeachment crisis (2024-2025) created uncertainty

military.summary: Firmly in opposing alignments. South Korea is a US treaty ally with 28,500 US troops on its soil. China objects to US military presence and THAAD. China-North Korea alignment means China is, in effect, backing South Korea's primary adversary. No arms transfers or joint exercises.
incidents: China opposes THAAD missile defense system as anti-China
China backs North Korea diplomatically, limiting UN sanctions effectiveness
Chinese military exercises and overflights near Korean peninsula

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment declining. Chinese investment in Korea is limited. Korean conglomerates (Samsung, SK, LG, Hyundai) have large China operations but are diversifying to Vietnam, India. China informally targeted Korean brands (Lotte, Hyundai) during THAAD dispute. Investment flows becoming more cautious.

energy.summary: South Korea imports Chinese solar panels and battery materials. Some LNG trade. South Korea is reducing rare earth dependency on China. Chinese-Korean competition in battery supply chains intensifying.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation in materials science, display technology, and chemistry. Korean universities maintain Chinese partnerships. Growing scrutiny as Korea aligns more closely with US technology frameworks. Joint publications declining from previous highs.
